seoTitle: Ophir vs Orange — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Ophir and Orange. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ophir and Orange.
heading: Ophir vs Orange — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ophir, New South Wales and Orange, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Orange has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Ophir (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ophir or Orange safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ophir has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Orange scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ophir scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Orange scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ophir or Orange?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Orange has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
